name: sdlc-plugin
description: Software Development Lifecycle skills, bundled. Eight skills covering idea-to-backlog, implementation, audit, and course-correction: /repo-bootstrap (new GitHub repo from scratch), /repo-level-up (add release/uat + release/prod branches, promotion workflows, secret scanning, branch-protection rulesets), /create-requirements (interactively elicit functional + non-functional requirements into docs/requirements/ — defaults to README.md as the source, asks for an alternative if missing), /confirm-requirements (validate and advance the status of existing requirements), /plan-from-requirements (turn the requirements into a staged plan of small-batch GitHub issues with milestones, labels, and human-required tasks front-loaded), /implement-task (heavyweight end-to-end implementation of one GitHub issue via a multi-persona orchestration — Principal Engineer, QA Engineer, Cloud Architect, Test Automation Engineer, Project Manager, and Work Checker — with audit-trail comments on the issue and a diligent PM bounce-back gate), /check-work (standalone 'check your work carefully' audit pass over a slice of recent work — uncommitted changes, an unpushed branch, a commit range, a PR, or a path — same audit logic as the Work Checker persona inside /implement-task, invocable on its own), /rework (when an implementation-time discovery invalidates the plan — assertively cleans up stale requirements and issues, with a documented audit trail).
